summary: Frontend-only manual E2E testing of Chekou Ganhou Expo Web app via mobile viewport (390x844). Covered authentication for all 4 mock accounts, auth-gate routing, full client order flow (Tosta 2 → review → checkout with coupons → simulated payment → tracking with 4-digit code), pharmacy warning, history/coupons/promotions, driver flow (accept → advance status → actual value → photos → confirmation code), pending driver routing, partner signup form, signup of new user, and admin panel (tabs, drivers approval/level, establishments CRUD, products). Almost all flows work; found 2 real bugs.

ui_bugs.issue: After entering CORRECT 4-digit code, the order is set to 'Entregue' in storage but the screen does NOT navigate back to /driver/home. Root cause: uses Alert.alert(...,[{onPress: () => router.replace('/driver/home')}]) — on react-native-web Alert.alert is mapped to window.alert and the per-button onPress callbacks are NOT executed. Same pattern likely affects other success Alerts (acceptOrder, saveActualValue, sendInvoice, sendGoods, partner-signup submit success).

Driver level and operational limit are NOT displayed anywhere on the driver home. `levelInfo` is computed (line 45) and `levelStrip`/`levelTitle`/`levelHint` styles are defined (lines 230-236) but no JSX renders them. Spec requires showing 'Nível 2 — Parceiro confiável' and 'Limite operacional R$ 150'. testID 'driver-level' / 'driver-operational-limit' missing.

issues: Console warning: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated, use boxShadow (non-blocking, react-native-web 0.20+ deprecation)

action_items: Fix driver delivery confirmation navigation: do not rely on Alert.alert button callbacks on web. Either (a) call router.replace('/driver/home') BEFORE Alert.alert, (b) use Platform.OS check and replace immediately on web, or (c) implement a custom modal with proper button handlers. Apply the same fix to acceptOrder, saveActualValue, sendInvoice, sendGoods, partner-signup success.

Render the driver level strip on /driver/home so 'Nível 2 — Parceiro confiável' and 'Limite operacional R$ 150' are visible (use already-defined levelStrip/levelTitle/levelHint styles + DRIVER_LEVELS[level]).
Optional: add testIDs for the driver level (e.g. driver-level-name, driver-level-limit) to enable automated assertions.
critical_code_review_comments: Alert.alert with onPress callbacks does not work reliably on react-native-web — avoid coupling navigation/state changes to alert button callbacks.
driver/home.tsx has dead style definitions (levelStrip, levelTitle, levelHint) — either render the component or remove the styles.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data created. localStorage is per-browser-context; each browser_automation invocation is a fresh context so cross-session persistence cannot be verified across separate scripts. Within a single script, client→logout→driver login correctly shares the order created by the client.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All test credentials in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Use EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. Each playwright invocation gets fresh localStorage, so test multi-role flows in a single script (login as client → create order → logout → login as driver → finish in same script). Use page.on('dialog', lambda d: d.accept()) to dismiss web alerts. Admin tab testIDs are capitalized: admin-tab-Resumo, admin-tab-Pedidos, admin-tab-Motoristas, admin-tab-Usuários, admin-tab-Lojas, admin-tab-Produtos, admin-tab-Cupons, admin-tab-Disputas.
rca_of_issue: Driver delivery navigation bug — Reproduction: 1) Log in cliente@chekou.com, create order, pay, copy 4-digit code from tracking page. 2) Logout, log in entregador@chekou.com. 3) Open available order, accept, click 'Avançar' until status 'A caminho do cliente', enter actual value, send photos. 4) Enter correct code in driver-code-input and click driver-confirm-delivery. Expected: redirect to /driver/home with delivery marked Entregue. Actual: stays on /driver/order/{id}. Order IS updated to status 'Entregue' in storage; only the navigation in the Alert.alert OK button callback never fires because react-native-web maps Alert.alert to window.alert and ignores per-button onPress. Mitigation: replace `Alert.alert('Entrega concluída!', '...', [{ text: 'OK', onPress: () => router.replace('/driver/home') }])` with: `router.replace('/driver/home'); Alert.alert('Entrega concluída!', 'Taxa liberada para seu saldo.');` (navigate first, then notify).
